Oracle Events
OE 1: Retrieve the full conversation history of channel C009 (#root-cause-aws-spike) using conversations_history, or search using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "AWS" or "cost spike" or "root cause" (or similar). Discovers: Samira's investigation kickoff (March AWS bill $42,870 vs ~$14,300 baseline, ~3x growth), Lena's CloudWatch analysis (autoscaling group moveops-api-prod scaled from 4 to 18 EC2 instances, 47,000 S3 document uploads in 7 days vs ~2,000/month normal, RDS connections surged from 50 to 340, slow queries in the relocation matching algorithm), root cause confirmed as Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding (~1,200 users starting March 21), and Samira's formal RCA summary posted to the channel.
OE 2: Search Slack channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "P0" or "P1" or "cost alert" or "budget tags" or "AWS follow-up" (or similar), or retrieve history using conversations_history on C003. Discovers: Samira's prioritized engineering task breakdown P0 (This Week): (1) CloudWatch cost alert thresholds at 50%, 100%, 200% of monthly baseline with Slack integration to #finance, (2) budget tags for all key AWS resources by customer ID; P1 (Next 2 Weeks): (3) infrastructure cost estimator tool for sales, (4) slow query optimization in relocation matching algorithm; P2 (This Quarter): (5) autoscaling policy review including spot instances, (6) S3 lifecycle policies for onboarding documents.
OE 3: Search Slack channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "CloudWatch" or "alarms" or "budget tags" or "slow queries" filtered to Lena (or similar). Discovers: Lena's response committing to P0 items she began setting up CloudWatch alarms for cost thresholds, budget tags to be added via Terraform update tagged by customer ID, and offered to fix the slow RDS queries concurrently. She stated items 1 and 2 would be ready by Wednesday. No subsequent message from Lena confirming completion was posted.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "AWS" or "root cause" or "cost spike" or "Vantage Distributed" (or similar). Discovers: Marcus Thorne's escalation email to Samira and Elena (CC'd) reporting the $42,870 March AWS bill with breakdown (EC2: $28,450, S3: $5,500, RDS: $8,920). Also discovers Samira's formal RCA email (to Marcus and Elena, CC Lena, Julian, Alejandro) with full root cause analysis confirming Vantage Distributed onboarding as the cause. Also discovers Elena's reply with directives: CloudWatch cost alerts are non-negotiable this week, infrastructure cost estimator tool needed before next enterprise pitch, pricing model review meeting to be scheduled, and any deal exceeding 500 employees requires engineering cost approval.
OE 5: Search Slack channel C004 (#executive) using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "AWS" or "cost" or "pricing model" (or similar). Discovers: Elena's executive directive (April 1) summarizing her email directives — cost alerting required, pricing model review needed, and sign-off process for engineering costs on large deals. Elena states the team will meet on Monday to finalize everything.
OE 6: Search Linear for issues related to the AWS investigation follow-up using linear_list_issues with queries related to "AWS" or "CloudWatch" or "cost alert" or "budget tags" or "infrastructure" (or similar), and also search by assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom. Discovers: NO Linear tickets exist for any of the P0/P1/P2 AWS follow-up items. Lena's 7 assigned tickets (ENG-205, ENG-206, ENG-207, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-187) are all non-AWS-related (6 ExpenseBot, 1 invoice safety check). The AWS cost control work was committed to in Slack and email but never formally tracked in Linear.
OE 7: Search CRM for Terraform Digital using crm_search_companies with name "Vantage" or "Vantage Distributed" (or similar), or crm_search_deals with dealname "Terraform" (or similar), then crm_search_deals with the company_id. Discovers: company-terraform-digital (Vantage Distributed), the original $1.85M enterprise deal (deal-terraform-digital-001, closedwon), and the first relocation batch deal (deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, $7,500, qualification stage) covering 3 employees — Priya Dasgupta moving May 5, Samantha Obi May 8, Marcus Liu May 12. Account manager: Mina Hashimoto.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for the AWS vendor bill using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill with vendor reference "vendor-aws-001" or search for "Amazon Web Services" (or similar). Discovers: bill-aws-march-001 for the March 2026 AWS bill showing the $42,870 total that triggered the investigation.
OE 9: Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Samira" or "Samira Tariq" (or similar). Discovers: samira.tariq@moveops.com, Engineering Manager.
OE 10: Create a Linear issue (linear_create_issue) on team_engineering for the CloudWatch cost alert thresholds (P0 item 1), assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, with description covering: alert thresholds at 50%/100%/200% of monthly baseline, Slack integration to #finance, committed by Lena on April 1 with Wednesday ETA, no confirmation of completion found. Priority P1 or Urgent given Terraform's May relocation batch.
OE 11: Create a Linear issue (linear_create_issue) on team_engineering for the AWS budget tag implementation (P0 item 2), assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, with description covering: Terraform-based tagging of all key AWS resources by customer ID for per-customer cost allocation. Committed by Lena on April 1 alongside the CloudWatch alerts.
OE 12: Create a Linear issue (linear_create_issue) on team_engineering for the relocation matching algorithm slow query optimization (P1 item 4), assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, with description covering: 3 queries in the relocation matching algorithm not utilizing indexes correctly, identified by Lena during the AWS spike investigation, contributing to the RDS connection spike from 50 to 340.
OE 13: Add a comment (linear_create_comment) on each newly created ticket documenting the source (Slack investigation channel, Samira's P0/P1/P2 breakdown, Lena's commitment message), the evidence of work status (committed but no completion confirmation found in Slack or email), and the urgency context (Terraform's first relocation batch starting May 5).
OE 14: Send an email (send_email) from lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com covering: (a) P0 status CloudWatch alarms and budget tags were committed April 1 but no completion confirmation found, tickets now created; (b) P1 status slow query optimization committed but untracked, ticket now created; infrastructure cost estimator tool (P1 item 3) has no owner or ticket; (c) P2 items autoscaling policy review and S3 lifecycle policies remain unstarted with no tickets; (d) risk context Terraform's first relocation batch (3 employees, May 5-12) will generate self-service portal traffic similar to the original spike; (e) recommendation on what to prioritize before the May ramp.
OE 15: Post a message (conversations_add_message) in channel C003 (#engineering) summarizing: AWS RCA follow-up items have been tracked in Linear P0 and P1 tickets created, status of each item noted, Terraform's May batch approaching as the deadline for infrastructure readiness. Team should review and update ticket status if any items were completed but not confirmed in Slack.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
